managedCourses.title: Cyber Security Phishing Awareness Trainings
managedCourses.subtitle: Cyber Security Phishing Awareness Training equips individuals with the knowledge to identify, prevent, and respond to phishing attacks, helping protect sensitive data and strengthen organizational security.

items.label: Video — How to use this course & navigation
items.about:
This course provides a comprehensive introduction to phishing, one of the most prevalent and dangerous cyber threats in today’s digital world. It explores how cyber attackers impersonate trusted organizations to deceive individuals into sharing sensitive information such as passwords, banking credentials, and personal data.

Through real-world examples and visual demonstrations, the course explains how phishing attacks are crafted to appear legitimate across emails, websites, and digital notifications. It also highlights the psychological manipulation techniques—such as urgency, fear, and enticing offers—that attackers use to exploit human behavior.

Designed for learners of all levels, this course builds essential awareness around phishing risks and equips individuals with the knowledge needed to recognize and respond to potential threats effectively.
learningOutcomes: Understand the concept of phishing and how it operates in different forms.
Identify common characteristics of phishing emails, links, and fake websites.
Recognize psychological tactics used by attackers to manipulate users.
Analyze real-life phishing examples to distinguish between legitimate and fraudulent communications.
Understand the risks and consequences of falling victim to phishing attacks.
Apply best practices to detect, avoid, and respond to phishing attempts.
Develop safer online habits to protect personal and organizational data.

curriculum.title: Module 2 : Anatomy of a Phishing Email

items.label: Video — Anatomy of a Phishing Email
items.about:
This module focuses on the anatomy of a phishing email and explains how attackers carefully design fraudulent messages to appear legitimate and trustworthy. It breaks down the key components of phishing emails, including the sender’s address, subject line, greeting, message body, malicious links, and attachments.

Learners will explore how each element is strategically crafted to manipulate recipients into taking actions such as clicking harmful links or sharing sensitive information. The module also highlights how attackers impersonate trusted organizations like banks, companies, and online platforms to gain credibility.

In addition, it explains the psychological techniques—such as urgency, fear, and curiosity—that are commonly used to influence user behavior. This module aims to build a deeper understanding of how phishing emails are structured, enabling users to detect threats more effectively.
learningOutcomes: Understand the structure and key components of a phishing email.
Identify suspicious sender addresses and misleading subject lines.
Analyze email content to detect signs of fraud or manipulation.
Recognize malicious links and unsafe attachments.
Understand how attackers impersonate trusted organizations.
Identify psychological tactics used in phishing emails.
Evaluate emails critically before taking any action.
Improve ability to detect and prevent phishing attacks

curriculum.title: Module 3 : How to Identify Phishing Emails

items.label: Video — Scenario-based lesson
items.about:
This module provides a practical approach to identifying phishing emails using five quick and effective verification checks. It focuses on simple yet powerful techniques that users can apply before interacting with any suspicious message.

The module emphasizes the importance of examining the sender’s email address, as attackers often disguise malicious domains behind familiar names. It also highlights the risks associated with unexpected attachments, particularly files like .zip, .exe, or unfamiliar documents from unknown sources.

Learners will understand how phishing emails often contain spelling and grammatical errors, which can serve as warning signs. The module further explains how attackers use urgent or threatening language to pressure users into acting quickly without proper verification. Additionally, it introduces the practice of hovering over links to reveal their actual destination before clicking.

Overall, this module is designed to equip users with quick, actionable checks that significantly reduce the risk of falling victim to phishing attacks.
learningOutcomes: Apply five quick verification checks to assess suspicious emails.
Identify fake or misleading sender email addresses.
Recognize risks associated with unexpected or suspicious attachments.
Detect spelling and grammatical errors as potential red flags.
Understand how urgency and threats are used to manipulate users.
Safely inspect links by checking their actual destination before clicking.
Make informed decisions before interacting with email content.
Enhance overall email security awareness and reduce phishing risks.

curriculum.title: Module 4 — Standards & compliance (Cyber Security Phishing Awareness Trainings)

items.label: Video — Regulatory and policy context
items.about:
This module focuses on how organizations effectively respond to phishing attacks by establishing clear roles, responsibilities, and defense strategies across all levels. It presents phishing as a critical social engineering threat and highlights the importance of rapid detection, containment, and mitigation to safeguard sensitive information.

The module outlines the responsibilities of key stakeholders within an organization, including leadership, security teams, managers, employees, and support functions such as HR and legal. It explains how coordinated efforts ensure an efficient and timely response to phishing incidents.

Additionally, the module introduces the incident response lifecycle, covering stages such as identification, response, recovery, and continuous improvement. It also emphasizes preventive measures like security awareness training, email filtering systems, multi-factor authentication, and phishing simulations to strengthen organizational resilience.

Overall, this module aims to build a proactive and well-coordinated cybersecurity approach to effectively manage and prevent phishing threats.
learningOutcomes: Understand phishing as a social engineering threat at an organizational level.
Identify roles and responsibilities of key stakeholders in responding to phishing attacks.
Explain the incident response lifecycle for phishing incidents.
Recognize the importance of rapid detection, containment, and mitigation.
Understand the role of employees as the first line of defense.
Apply preventive measures such as awareness training, email filtering, and multi-factor authentication.
Evaluate how coordination across teams improves incident response effectiveness.
Contribute to building a proactive cybersecurity culture within an organization.
